blocks.content: Friday. The last day of the week. I walked to school with my bag over one shoulder and the Thursday steps still sitting somewhere in the back of everything.
There was a thing Mr. Cole said in the car that I kept coming back to: I'm not always going to pull up at the right time. Get in the habit of not needing me to.

I kept thinking about what that habit actually looked like. Not avoiding the side exit. Not waiting to see if things would be okay. Moving through the day like someone who knew how to handle the thing that hadn't happened yet.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: You look like you're thinking something heavy.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: What would it look like if I wasn't?

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Like you now but slightly lighter.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I'm okay. Just been thinking about what Mr. Cole said. About not needing him to pull up.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Yeah. I've been thinking about that too.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Daniel's not done. You know that right. Him backing off isn't him stopping. It's him re-routing.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: What do you want to do about it?
That was the right question. Not what do you think will happen — what do you WANT to do.
